---
title: "Drifting and Chart Transport"
author: "Nicholas Lyu"
date: last-modified
title-block-banner: false
---

Summary of research progress in the last month.
We isolated some drifting problems and experimented with
chart transport methods. Concludes with some
preliminary MNIST experiments and takeaways.

**The page might take a while to load**

## Drifting mode collapse

We apply MLP-drifting to a **bimodal Gaussian in 2D** below.
This demonstrates
[mode collapse from disjoint initialization]{style="color: blue;"}.
After converging to a mode, the model has almost no incentive to
cover the other mode.

<div style="text-align: center; height: 578px; overflow: hidden;">
<iframe src="writeup/assets/drifting_revkl.html"
        width="720" height="770"
        style="border: none; transform: scale(0.75); transform-origin: top center;">
</iframe>
</div>

Generally, model-sample drifting without importance sampling
all suffer from this reverse-KL mode-collapse problem.

## Chart transport

The main idea of our proposal is to _build an autoencoder (chart)
between the latent and model spaces_. This way, we can **drift the data latent** towards the
model latent (prior) to optimize forward-KL.
Sample space distributions match when:

1. Latent-space distributions match.
We supervise this using a latent-space score critic which provides the latent drift field.
2. Sample-to-latent encoding is reconstructible.


Mechanically, the <span style="color: rgb(14, 120, 56); font-weight: 600;">latent score field</span> (<span style="color: rgb(14, 120, 56);">green arrow</span> right) guides the model samples (<span style="color: rgb(208, 51, 58);">red</span> and <span style="color: rgb(30, 100, 200);">blue</span> right)
towards the <span style="color: rgb(90, 90, 90); font-weight: 600;">model latent distribution</span> (<span style="color: rgb(90, 90, 90);">black</span> right).

This induces the <span style="color: rgb(208, 51, 58); font-weight: 600;">data</span> (<span style="color: rgb(208, 51, 58);">red</span>) and <span style="color: rgb(17, 24, 39); font-weight: 600;">sample</span> (<span style="color: rgb(17, 24, 39);">black</span>)
distributions (left) to match.

<style>
.scaled-plotly-frame {
  max-width: 100%;
  margin: 0 auto;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.scaled-plotly-frame > iframe {
  display: block;
  border: 0;
  transform-origin: top left;
}
</style>

<script>
window.fitScaledIframeContainer = function(frame) {
  const iframe = frame.querySelector("iframe");
  const sourceWidth = Number(frame.dataset.sourceWidth);
  const sourceHeight = Number(frame.dataset.sourceHeight);
  const requestedWidth = Number(frame.dataset.width);

  if (!iframe || !sourceWidth || !sourceHeight) {
    return;
  }

  if (requestedWidth) {
    frame.style.width = `${requestedWidth}px`;
  }

  const displayWidth = frame.clientWidth || Math.min(requestedWidth || sourceWidth, sourceWidth);
  const scale = displayWidth / sourceWidth;

  frame.style.height = `${Math.ceil(sourceHeight * scale)}px`;
  iframe.style.width = `${sourceWidth}px`;
  iframe.style.height = `${sourceHeight}px`;
  iframe.style.transform = `scale(${scale})`;
};

window.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const frames = document.querySelectorAll(".scaled-plotly-frame");
  frames.forEach(window.fitScaledIframeContainer);

  if ("ResizeObserver" in window) {
    const observer = new ResizeObserver((entries) => {
      entries.forEach((entry) => window.fitScaledIframeContainer(entry.target));
    });
    frames.forEach((frame) => observer.observe(frame));
  } else {
    window.addEventListener("resize", () => frames.forEach(window.fitScaledIframeContainer));
  }

  document.querySelectorAll("details").forEach((d) => {
    d.addEventListener("toggle", () => {
      d.querySelectorAll(".scaled-plotly-frame").forEach(window.fitScaledIframeContainer);
    });
  });
});
</script>

<div class="scaled-plotly-frame" data-width="720" data-source-width="1416" data-source-height="766">
<iframe src="writeup/assets/chart_transport_bimodal_fix.html"
        width="1416" height="766"
        scrolling="no"
        onload="window.fitScaledIframeContainer(this.parentElement);">
</iframe>
</div>

For reference, we also ran a flow-matching baseline.

<details>
<summary><strong>Flow-matching bimodal baseline</strong></summary>

<div class="scaled-plotly-frame" data-width="720" data-source-width="1416" data-source-height="766">
<iframe src="writeup/assets/flow_matching_bimodal.html"
        width="1416" height="766"
        scrolling="no"
        onload="window.fitScaledIframeContainer(this.parentElement);">
</iframe>
</div>

</details>

## Chart transport challenge: space-filling

We realized that latent distribution matching becomes fragile
**when the data has low intrinsic dimension**. In the experiment below,
we generate multimodal Gaussian data on a **2D manifold in 3D ambient space**.

Bad things happen when we try to warp a thin, 2D data latent surface into
a thick target latent distribution.

Note how the chart (autoencoder) struggles to warp the 2D data latent
into 3D latent target. In general, space-filling using low-dimensional objects
invite ill-conditioning. This appears to be a fundamental challenge with our method.

### Partial solution: stochastic encoding

To address the problem above, we tried using a **stochastic encoder**.
Each data sample maps to a "fiber" in latent space.
We still match sample-space distributions when
the marginal latent-fiber distribution match, and
**distinct fibers are reconstructible**.

## MNIST experiments

<details>
<summary><strong>Flow-matching MNIST baseline</strong></summary>

Flow matching with the same MLP architecture, 50 Euler steps.

<div style="text-align: center; height: 443px; overflow: hidden;">
<iframe src="writeup/assets/flow_matching_mnist.html"
        width="540" height="590"
        style="border: none; transform: scale(0.75); transform-origin: top center;">
</iframe>
</div>

</details>


Real-world data likely lies on a manifold with some noise.
Still, generation quality is still latent-dimension dependent.
Stochastic encoding mitigates the failure mode (see below),
but generated samples are still the best when the latent dimension
matches the intrinsic data manifold dimension, and when decoding
is deterministic.

### 12-dimensional latent

#### Deterministic encoding

<div class="scaled-plotly-frame" data-width="520" data-source-width="520" data-source-height="570">
<iframe src="writeup/assets/deterministic_chart_transport_mnist_d12.html"
        width="520" height="570"
        scrolling="no"
        onload="window.fitScaledIframeContainer(this.parentElement);">
</iframe>
</div>

#### Stochastic encoding
<div class="scaled-plotly-frame" data-width="520" data-source-width="520" data-source-height="570">
<iframe src="writeup/assets/stochastic_chart_transport_mnist_d12.html"
        width="520" height="570"
        scrolling="no"
        onload="window.fitScaledIframeContainer(this.parentElement);">
</iframe>
</div>

### 32-dimensional latent

#### Deterministic

<div class="scaled-plotly-frame" data-width="520" data-source-width="520" data-source-height="570">
<iframe src="writeup/assets/deterministic_chart_transport_mnist_d32.html"
        width="520" height="570"
        scrolling="no"
        onload="window.fitScaledIframeContainer(this.parentElement);">
</iframe>
</div>

#### Stochastic

<div class="scaled-plotly-frame" data-width="520" data-source-width="520" data-source-height="570">
<iframe src="writeup/assets/stochastic_chart_transport_mnist_d32.html"
        width="520" height="570"
        scrolling="no"
        onload="window.fitScaledIframeContainer(this.parentElement);">
</iframe>
</div>

#### 128-dimensional latent

#### Deterministic

<div class="scaled-plotly-frame" data-width="520" data-source-width="520" data-source-height="570">
<iframe src="writeup/assets/deterministic_chart_transport_mnist_d128.html"
        width="520" height="570"
        scrolling="no"
        onload="window.fitScaledIframeContainer(this.parentElement);">
</iframe>
</div>

#### Stochastic

<div class="scaled-plotly-frame" data-width="520" data-source-width="520" data-source-height="570">
<iframe src="writeup/assets/stochastic_chart_transport_mnist_d128.html"
        width="520" height="570"
        scrolling="no"
        onload="window.fitScaledIframeContainer(this.parentElement);">
</iframe>
</div>
